decisions.summary: Two of the three issue concerns are already fixed on main (commit ab8cd6f70). Plan only the remaining gap: cloister/prompts/{review,test,planning,resume-work}.md still lack the {{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}} block.

Apply the block to all four cloister prompts (planning, review, test, resume-work) per the literal text of the issue's Fix #3, even though three of them are not currently rendered by production code paths. Tests already exercise them via prompts.test.ts, and the block is a no-op when TLDR_AVAILABLE is unset.

Only the planning prompt needs a production wire-up (spawn-planning-session.ts must pass TLDR_AVAILABLE). The review/test/resume-work prompts have no production render site to wire — adding to optional frontmatter is enough.

Do NOT delete the stale cloister/prompts/{review,test,resume-work}.md files in this issue. They are still exercised by src/lib/cloister/__tests__/prompts.test.ts (rendering smoke + MEMORY_CONTEXT propagation); deleting them is a separate cleanup with test-update cost that exceeds this issue's scope.

hazards.summary: renderPrompt strictly validates that every variable passed in is declared in the prompt's 'requires' or 'optional' frontmatter (src/lib/cloister/prompts.ts:167-181). If TLDR_AVAILABLE is passed at a render site but missing from the prompt's 'optional:' list, rendering will throw.
hazards.mitigation: Add TLDR_AVAILABLE to the 'optional:' list of every prompt that gets the block, even if no production site passes it yet. Update spawn-planning-session.ts to pass the flag for planning.md.

hazards.summary: prompts.test.ts renders review/test/planning/work prompts (lines 269-339) for MEMORY_CONTEXT propagation. Adding TLDR_AVAILABLE to optional must not require new test variables — Mustache renders `{{#FLAG}}…{{/FLAG}}` as empty when FLAG is unset, and renderPrompt does not require optional vars to be present.
hazards.mitigation: Verify by running the existing prompts.test.ts after the change. No test changes should be needed unless a new assertion is added to prove the conditional rendering works.

hazards.summary: The bulk of the original PAN-1132 work shipped on main as commit ab8cd6f70. Re-implementing what is already merged would be wasted work or revert recent fixes.
hazards.mitigation: Before changing any file, verify the file's current state. tldr-daemon.ts, sync.ts (LEGACY_SKILL_NAMES), sync-sources/skills/pan-tldr/, roles/plan.md, roles/test.md, and roles/review-{security,correctness,performance,requirements}.md are all already correct — do not touch them.

hazards.summary: The PAN-1132 verification criterion 'pan admin tldr status correctly reports running/stopped after CLI exits' was verified during planning (this workspace at PID 291042 reports running, healthy). Subsequent code touches should not regress the pidfile-based readDaemonState() logic in tldr-daemon.ts:216-246.
hazards.mitigation: Run `pan admin tldr status` before and after the work as a regression check.

hazards.summary: src/lib/cloister/prompts/work.md already uses {{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}. The block text should follow the same shape (clear use-cases, MCP tool list, when-to-read-full) so reviewers don't see drift across prompts.
hazards.mitigation: Use the existing work.md TLDR block as the template; tailor the framing to each role (planning = broad exploration, review = code reading, test = failure diagnosis, resume-work = pick up mid-task).

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1132: TLDR: fix broken status reporting + expose to work agents

Auto-mode planning completed. Discovered that commit ab8cd6f70 on main already fixes issue concerns #1 (daemon pidfile) and #2 (pan-tldr skill restore), plus partially fixes #3 by adding TLDR sections to roles/{plan,test,review-*}.md. Scoped remaining work to the literal item #3 — adding {{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}} blocks to the four cloister/prompts/*.md templates listed in the issue body.
